title: codonorhiza fastigiata
intro: Codonorhiza fastigiata, also known as the "Sticktail" or "Rat's Tail," is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical forests of Asia. It is commonly used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Codonorhiza fastigiata include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Codonorhiza fastigiata can be made by boiling the roots and leaves in water, which can then be used as a tea or applied topically to affected areas.
